html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 钦定四库全书 - A formal title indicating that the work is officially sanctioned and part of the imperial collection.
  • 子部十二 - Refers to the twelfth category in the traditional Chinese classification system for books, which is the 'Miscellaneous' category.
  • 提要 - A brief summary or introduction, often used in bibliographies.
  • 北梦琐言二十卷 - Refers to the title and number of volumes of the book.
  • 孙光宪 - The author of the book.
  • 字孟文 - The courtesy name of the author.
  • 自号葆光子 - The author's self-given name.
  • 十国春秋 - A historical record of the Ten Kingdoms period.
  • 贵平人 - Indicates that the author is from Guiping.
  • 富春 - Refers to the place of origin, which is actually a reference to the author's clan name.
  • 生自岷峨 - Indicates that the author was born in the region around Mount Emei and Mount Min.
  • 富春葢举郡望也 - Explains that 'Fuchun' is a reference to the author's clan name, not his place of birth.
  • 仕唐为陵州判官 - Describes the author's official position during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 旋依荆南高季兴为从事 - Indicates that the author later served under Gao Jixing in Jingnan.
  • 后劝高继冲以三州归宋 - Describes the author's role in persuading Gao Jichong to surrender three states to the Song Dynasty.
  • 太祖嘉之 - Indicates that Emperor Taizu of Song praised the author.
  • 授黄州刺史 - Describes the author's appointment as the governor of Huangzhou.
  • 以终五代史荆南世家 - Indicates that the author's career is recorded in the 'Jingnan Biographies' of the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
  • 旧以为五代人者悮矣 - Corrects a previous misunderstanding that the author lived during the Five Dynasties period.
  • 所着有荆台集橘斋集玩笔佣集巩湖编玩蚕书续通厯等书 - Lists the other works written by the author.
  • 自宋代已散佚 - Indicates that these works have been lost since the Song Dynasty.
  • 惟是书独传于后 - States that only this book has survived.
  • 曰北梦琐言者 - Explains the title of the book.
  • 以左传称田于江南之梦 - Refers to a passage in the Zuo Zhuan where it mentions a dream about tilling land in the south of the river.
  • 而荆州在江北 - Indicates that Jingzhou is located north of the Yangtze River.
  • 故以命名 - Explains why the book is named 'North Dream Trifles.'
  • 所载皆唐及五代士大夫逸事 - Describes the content of the book, which includes anecdotes about officials from the Tang and Five Dynasties periods.
  • 每条多载某人 - Indicates that each entry often mentions a specific person.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钦定四库全书 - The 'Qinding Siku Quanshu' is a massive collection of Chinese literature commissioned by the Qing dynasty.
  • 子部十二 - The 'Zi' category is one of the four main categories in the traditional Chinese classification system for books, which also includes 'Jing' (classics), 'Shi' (histories), and 'Ji' (collections).
  • 提要 - A 'tiyao' is a brief summary or introduction, often used in bibliographies.
  • 孙光宪 - A notable historian and writer from the late Tang and early Song periods.
  • 字孟文 - The courtesy name 'Mengwen' is a common practice in Chinese culture.
  • 自号葆光子 - The author's self-given name 'Bao Guangzi' is a reflection of his personal philosophy or aspirations.
  • 十国春秋 - A historical record of the Ten Kingdoms period, which is a part of Chinese history between the Tang and Song dynasties.
  • 贵平人 - Indicates the author's place of origin, which is Guiping.
  • 富春 - A reference to the author's clan name, not his place of birth.
  • 生自岷峨 - Indicates that the author was born in the region around Mount Emei and Mount Min, which are famous mountains in Sichuan.
  • 富春葢举郡望也 - Explains that 'Fuchun' is a reference to the author's clan name, not his place of birth.
  • 仕唐为陵州判官 - Describes the author's official position during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 旋依荆南高季兴为从事 - Indicates that the author later served under Gao Jixing in Jingnan.
  • 后劝高继冲以三州归宋 - Describes the author's role in persuading Gao Jichong to surrender three states to the Song Dynasty.
  • 太祖嘉之 - Indicates that Emperor Taizu of Song praised the author.
  • 授黄州刺史 - Describes the author's appointment as the governor of Huangzhou.
  • 以终五代史荆南世家 - Indicates that the author's career is recorded in the 'Jingnan Biographies' of the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
  • 旧以为五代人者悮矣 - Corrects a previous misunderstanding that the author lived during the Five Dynasties period.
  • 所着有荆台集橘斋集玩笔佣集巩湖编玩蚕书续通厯等书 - Lists the other works written by the author.
  • 自宋代已散佚 - Indicates that these works have been lost since the Song Dynasty.
  • 惟是书独传于后 - States that only this book has survived.
  • 曰北梦琐言者 - Explains the title of the book.
  • 以左传称田于江南之梦 - Refers to a passage in the Zuo Zhuan where it mentions a dream about tilling land in the south of the river.
  • 而荆州在江北 - Indicates that Jingzhou is located north of the Yangtze River.
  • 故以命名 - Explains why the book is named 'North Dream Trifles.'
  • 所载皆唐及五代士大夫逸事 - Describes the content of the book, which includes anecdotes about officials from the Tang and Five Dynasties periods.
  • 每条多载某人 - Indicates that each entry often mentions a specific person.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 所説以示有征 - Means 'what is said to show evidence,' emphasizing the need for substantiation.
  • 葢用杜阳编之例 - Refers to 'following the example of the *Du Yang* compilation,' a specific method or model.
  • 其记载颇猥叙次亦颇冗防 - Means 'its records are quite disordered and its sequence is also quite redundant,' indicating poor organization and excess.
  • 而遗文琐语往往可资考证 - Means 'and the remnants and trivial words can often be used for verification,' highlighting the potential value of minor details.
  • 故宋李昉等编太平广记多采其文 - Means 'Therefore, during the Song Dynasty, Li Fang and others compiled *Tai Ping Guang Ji* and drew much from its text,' indicating the text's influence.
  • 而此书未尝议及则语不甚诬可知矣 - Means 'but this book has never been criticized, so it is not very false, which can be known,' suggesting a lack of criticism implies some level of accuracy.
  • 脱悮殆不可读 - Means 'with many omissions and errors, making it nearly unreadable,' highlighting the poor quality of one edition.
  • 犹宋时陜西刋板差完整有绪 - Means 'still somewhat complete and orderly as the Song Dynasty's Shaanxi edition,' suggesting the quality of the new edition.
  • 故今以扬州本著录不用商氏本云 - Means 'Therefore, the Yangzhou edition is recorded, and the Shang edition is not used,' indicating the preferred edition.
  • 乾隆四十三年三月恭校上 - Means 'March of the 43rd year of Qianlong, respectfully submitted for proofreading,' indicating the date and context of the submission.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 杜阳编 - Refers to a specific compilation method or model.
  • 太平广记 - A large-scale collection of stories and anecdotes compiled during the Song Dynasty.
  • 读书志 - A catalog or record of books, specifically Chao Gongwu's work.
  • 光宪续通厯 - A historical work continued by Guangxian.
  • 马总之书 - Refers to a historical work by Ma Zong.
  • 黄巢 - A famous rebel leader during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 李茂贞 - A warlord during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 刘守光 - A warlord during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 按巴坚 - A historical figure, possibly a misspelling of 'An Baqin.'
  • 吴唐闽广吴越两蜀 - Refers to various regions and states during the Tang and Five Dynasties periods.
  • 太祖 - Refers to Emperor Taizu, the founder of the Song Dynasty.
  • 商维濬 - A Ming Dynasty scholar who compiled *Bai Hai*.
  • 稗海 - A collection of miscellaneous writings.
  • 元华亭孙道明 - A Yuan Dynasty scholar who collected a version of the text.
  • 乾隆四十三年 - The 43rd year of the Qianlong Emperor's reign (1778).

Proper Nouns

  • 杜阳编 - A specific compilation method or model.
  • 太平广记 - A large-scale collection of stories and anecdotes compiled during the Song Dynasty.
  • 读书志 - A catalog or record of books, specifically Chao Gongwu's work.
  • 光宪续通厯 - A historical work continued by Guangxian.
  • 马总之书 - Refers to a historical work by Ma Zong.
  • 黄巢 - A famous rebel leader during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 李茂贞 - A warlord during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 刘守光 - A warlord during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 按巴坚 - A historical figure, possibly a misspelling of 'An Baqin.'
  • 吴唐闽广吴越两蜀 - Refers to various regions and states during the Tang and Five Dynasties periods.
  • 太祖 - Refers to Emperor Taizu, the founder of the Song Dynasty.
  • 商维濬 - A Ming Dynasty scholar who compiled *Bai Hai*.
  • 稗海 - A collection of miscellaneous writings.
  • 元华亭孙道明 - A Yuan Dynasty scholar who collected a version of the text.
  • 乾隆四十三年 - The 43rd year of the Qianlong Emperor's reign (1778).

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 总纂官 - Title meaning 'Chief Compiler' or 'Chief Editor,' indicating the highest-ranking official overseeing the compilation of a work.
  • 总校官 - Title meaning 'Chief Proofreader' or 'Chief Examiner,' indicating the official responsible for the final review of the text.
  • 广明乱离 - Refers to the period of chaos and disorder during the reign of Emperor Zhaozong of Tang, specifically the 'Wanshou Rebellion' (880-884), which led to the decline of the Tang dynasty.
  • 秘籍亡散 - Means 'the loss of secret or rare books,' referring to the dispersal of valuable texts during times of turmoil.
  • 武宗已后 - Refers to the period after Emperor Wuzong of Tang (840-846), known for his persecution of Buddhism and other religious reforms.
  • 朝野遗芳 - Means 'the lingering fragrance of the court and countryside,' metaphorically referring to the cultural and intellectual heritage of the Tang dynasty.
  • 仆生自岷峨 - A humble expression meaning 'I was born in Min and E,' referring to the regions of Sichuan (Min) and Hubei (E).
  • 官于荆郢 - Means 'serving in Jing and Ying,' referring to the regions of Jingzhou and Yingzhou, both in Hubei province.
  • 咸京故事 - Refers to the 'ancient stories of Chang'an,' the capital of the Tang dynasty, symbolizing the rich cultural and historical legacy of the capital.
  • 面墙 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'to face a wall,' indicating a state of ignorance or lack of knowledge.
  • 游处之间 - Means 'in the midst of travel and residence,' referring to the author's experiences during his travels and stays in various places.
  • 专于博访 - Means 'diligently seeking out information,' indicating the author's dedication to gathering knowledge.
  • 逢故凤翔杨玭少尹 - Refers to meeting the former Deputy Prefect of Fengxiang, Yang Pin, indicating a specific encounter with a historical figure.
  • 多话秦中平时旧说 - Means 'he often spoke of the old customs and traditions of the Qin region,' referring to the cultural practices and historical narratives of the region.
  • 渚宫 - Refers to the 'Palace by the Lake,' a place in Jingzhou, Hubei, where the author encountered Yuan Cheng.
  • 元澄中允 - Refers to Yuan Cheng, a title meaning 'Middle Scholar' or 'Middle Official,' indicating a specific historical figure.
  • 欵狎笑语 - Means 'friendly and intimate conversations,' describing the nature of the interactions between the author and Yuan Cheng.
  • 符其说 - Means 'to match or confirm what was said,' indicating that the information provided by Yuan Cheng aligned with the author's previous knowledge.
  • 富春 - Refers to the region of Fuchun, known for its cultural and literary heritage.
  • 存好问之所宏益也 - Means 'to preserve the value of asking questions and thus expand one's knowledge,' emphasizing the importance of inquiry and learning.
  • 厥后 - A classical term meaning 'thereafter' or 'later on.'
  • 聆一事 - Means 'to hear or learn about one thing,' indicating the acquisition of new information.
  • 未敢孤信 - Means 'not daring to trust it alone,' indicating a cautious approach to new information.
  • 三复防校 - Means 'to carefully verify and cross-check multiple times,' emphasizing the importance of thorough verification.
  • 爰因公退 - Means 'after official duties were completed,' indicating the time when the author had free time to work on the compilation.
  • 咸取编连 - Means 'all were collected and compiled,' indicating the process of gathering and organizing the material.
  • 自唐至后唐梁蜀江南诸国 - Refers to the period from the Tang dynasty to the Later Tang, Later Liang, Shu, and Southern dynasties, covering a broad historical span.
  • 所得闻知者 - Means 'what was heard or known,' indicating the sources of information.
  • 皆附其末 - Means 'all were added to the end,' indicating the inclusion of additional information.
  • 凡纂得事成二十卷 - Means 'the total number of compiled events amounted to twenty volumes,' indicating the scope of the work.
  • 禹贡云云 - Refers to the *Yugong* (The Tribute of Yu), a chapter in the *Shangshu* (Book of Documents) that describes the geography and administration of ancient China.
  • 土梦作 - Means 'a dream of the land,' referring to a dream involving the land or territory.
  • 畋于江南之梦 - Means 'a dream of hunting in the south of the Yangtze River,' indicating a specific dream.
  • 鄙从事于荆江之北 - Means 'I have been serving in the north of the Jing River,' indicating the author's current location and occupation.
  • 题曰北梦琐言 - Means 'titled *North Dream Trifles*,' indicating the name of the work.
  • 琐细形言 - Means 'trivial and detailed speech,' indicating the nature of the content.
  • 大即可知也 - Means 'the main points can be understood,' indicating that the essence of the work is clear.
  • 虽非经纬之作 - Means 'although it is not a work of great depth,' indicating the modesty of the author.
  • 庶勉后进子孙 - Means 'to encourage future generations,' indicating the author's hope for the work's impact.
  • 俾希仰前事 - Means 'to make them look up to past events,' indicating the educational purpose of the work.
  • 亦丝麻中菅蒯也 - Means 'also like the coarse grass among silk and hemp,' indicating the humble nature of the work.
  • 通方者幸勿多诮焉 - Means 'may those who understand not criticize too harshly,' indicating the author's plea for understanding and leniency.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 总纂官 - Titles indicating the highest-ranking officials in the compilation and proofreading process, reflecting the hierarchical structure of the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 总校官 - Titles indicating the highest-ranking officials in the compilation and proofreading process, reflecting the hierarchical structure of the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 广明乱离 - A significant period of chaos and disorder in Chinese history, marking the decline of the Tang dynasty.
  • 秘籍亡散 - Reflects the historical context of the loss of valuable texts during times of political instability.
  • 武宗已后 - A period of significant religious and political reforms under Emperor Wuzong, known for his persecution of Buddhism.
  • 朝野遗芳 - Symbolizes the cultural and intellectual heritage of the Tang dynasty, which the author aims to preserve.
  • 岷峨 - Refers to the regions of Sichuan and Hubei, known for their rich cultural and natural landscapes.
  • 咸京故事 - Refers to the rich cultural and historical legacy of Chang'an, the capital of the Tang dynasty.
  • 面墙 - A metaphorical expression indicating a state of ignorance or lack of knowledge, often used in Confucian texts.
  • 游处之间 - Reflects the author's experiences during his travels and stays in various places, which influenced his compilation.
  • 专于博访 - Emphasizes the author's dedication to gathering knowledge, a common practice among scholars of the time.
  • 凤翔杨玭少尹 - A specific historical figure, Deputy Prefect of Fengxiang, who provided valuable information to the author.
  • 秦中平时旧说 - Refers to the cultural practices and historical narratives of the Qin region, which the author sought to document.
  • 渚宫 - A specific location in Jingzhou, Hubei, where the author encountered Yuan Cheng.
  • 元澄中允 - A specific historical figure, Middle Scholar or Middle Official, who provided additional information and insights.
  • 欵狎笑语 - Describes the nature of the interactions between the author and Yuan Cheng, indicating a friendly and intimate relationship.
  • 符其说 - Indicates that the information provided by Yuan Cheng aligned with the author's previous knowledge, adding credibility to the compilation.
  • 富春 - Known for its cultural and literary heritage, symbolizing a place of learning and wisdom.
  • 存好问之所宏益也 - Emphasizes the importance of inquiry and learning, a core value in Confucian thought.
  • 厥后 - Indicates the passage of time and the continuation of the author's work.
  • 聆一事 - Reflects the author's ongoing process of gathering new information.
  • 未敢孤信 - Indicates a cautious approach to new information, emphasizing the importance of verification.
  • 三复防校 - Emphasizes the importance of thorough verification, a common practice in scholarly work.
  • 爰因公退 - Indicates the time when the author had free time to work on the compilation, reflecting the demands of official duties.
  • 咸取编连 - Describes the process of gathering and organizing the material.
  • 自唐至后唐梁蜀江南诸国 - Covers a broad historical span.
  • 所得闻知者 - Indicates the sources of information.
  • 皆附其末 - Indicates the inclusion of additional information.
  • 凡纂得事成二十卷 - Indicates the scope of the work.
  • 禹贡云云 - Refers to the *Yugong* (The Tribute of Yu), a chapter in the *Shangshu* (Book of Documents) that describes the geography and administration of ancient China.
  • 土梦作 - Refers to a dream involving the land or territory.
  • 畋于江南之梦 - Indicates a specific dream.
  • 鄙从事于荆江之北 - Indicates the author's current location and occupation.
  • 题曰北梦琐言 - Indicates the name of the work.
  • 琐细形言 - Indicates the nature of the content.
  • 大即可知也 - Indicates that the essence of the work is clear.
  • 虽非经纬之作 - Indicates the modesty of the author.
  • 庶勉后进子孙 - Indicates the author's hope for the work's impact.
  • 俾希仰前事 - Indicates the educational purpose of the work.
  • 亦丝麻中菅蒯也 - Indicates the humble nature of the work.
  • 通方者幸勿多诮焉 - Indicates the author's plea for understanding and leniency.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 钦定四库全书 - Title indicating the text is part of the 'Complete Library in Four Branches of Literature,' a massive collection commissioned by the Qing dynasty.
  • 北梦琐言 - Title of the text, translating to 'Trifles of the Northern Dream,' suggesting a collection of anecdotes, stories, or historical notes.
  • 乡贡进士 - Title given to candidates who passed the local-level imperial examination and were eligible to take the higher-level exams.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐宣宗皇帝 - Emperor Xuanzong of the Tang dynasty (r. 846–859), known for his efforts to restore Confucian values and his interest in literature and governance.
  • 贡举 - The imperial examination system, which was the primary method for selecting government officials in imperial China.
  • 礼部侍郎 - A high-ranking official in the Ministry of Rites, responsible for overseeing the imperial examinations and other cultural matters.
  • 尧舜禹汤 - Legendary sage-kings of ancient China, often invoked as models of ideal rulership.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 欲优异之 - I wish to treat him with special favor.
  • 匡救必尽公忠 - Always offer advice with the utmost loyalty and integrity.
  • 亲戚之间人所难议 - It is difficult to discuss matters involving relatives.
  • 事事能如斯天下何忧不治 - If everything can be handled like this, what worries would there be in governing the world.
  • 再兴释教 - Reviving Buddhism again.
  • 所可存而勿论 - It is possible to preserve without discussing.
  • 唯出家者不得妄度也 - Only those who have taken monastic vows are not to be ordained indiscriminately.
  • 相国萧仿裴坦时为常侍谏议 - The Chancellor Xiao Fang and Pei Tan, who were serving as Attendants and Advisors at the time.
  • 臣等闻元 - We hear that the original.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鄭光 - A historical figure, the maternal uncle of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 云陽鄠縣 - Places in Shaanxi province, where the estates were located.
  • 宣宗 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, a ruler known for his policies and reforms.
  • 武宗 - Emperor Wuzong of Tang, who succeeded Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 懿宗 - Emperor Yizong of Tang, who succeeded Emperor Wuzong.
  • 釋教 - Buddhism, a major religion in China.
  • 相国 - A high-ranking official, often equivalent to a chancellor.
  • 常侍諫議 - Positions in the imperial court, Attendants and Advisors, who provided counsel to the emperor.

Proper Nouns

  • 鄭光 - Historical figure, maternal uncle of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 云陽鄠縣 - Places in Shaanxi province.
  • 宣宗 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 武宗 - Emperor Wuzong of Tang.
  • 懿宗 - Emperor Yizong of Tang.
  • 釋教 - Buddhism.
  • 相国 - High-ranking official, often equivalent to a chancellor.
  • 常侍諫議 - Positions in the imperial court, Attendants and Advisors.
  • 蕭仿 - Proper name, one of the officials.
  • 裴坦 - Proper name, one of the officials.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 祖之道用慈俭为先 - The way of the ancestor uses benevolence and frugality as the foremost principles.
  • 相沿百世作则千年 - Passed down through a hundred generations, serving as a model for a thousand years.
  • 至圣至明不可易也 - The most sage and the most wise cannot be changed.
  • 如佛者生于天竺 - As for the Buddha, born in Tianzhu (India).
  • 去彼王宫割爱中之至难 - Leaving the royal palace, cutting off the deepest attachments.
  • 取灭后之殊胜 - Achieving the extraordinary after Nirvana.
  • 名归象外理出尘中 - Fame returns beyond the realm, principles emerge from the mundane.
  • 非为帝王所能慕也 - Not something that emperors can aspire to.
  • 广引无益有损之义文多不录 - Extensively citing the meanings of what is futile and harmful, much of which is not recorded.
  • 文理婉顺与韩愈元和中上请除佛骨表不异也 - The style is gentle and smooth, similar to Han Yu's petition to remove the Buddha bones during the Yuanhe period.
  • 懿皇虽听览称奖竟不能止 - The wise emperor, though he listened and praised, could not stop it.
  • 末年迎佛骨才至京师俄而晏驾 - In his later years, when the Buddha bones arrived in the capital, he soon passed away.
  • 识者谓大丧之兆也 - Those who understand consider it a sign of great mourning.
  • 郑氏女庐墓 - The daughter of the Zheng family built a thatched hut over her father's grave.
  • 唐大中年兖州奏先差赴庆州行营押官郑神佐阵没 - In the Dazhong period of the Tang dynasty, the governor of Yan state reported that Zheng Shen Zuo, who was sent to guard the camp in Qingzhou, died in battle.
  • 其室女年二十四先亡父未行营已前许嫁右骁雄军健李元庆未受财礼 - His daughter, aged twenty-four, had already been betrothed to Li Yuanqing, a soldier in the Right Xiangxiong unit, but had not yet received the betrothal gifts.
  • 阿郑知父神佐阵没遂与李元庆休亲截髪往庆州北懐安镇亡父遗骸到兖州瑕丘县进贤乡与亡母合讫便于茔内筑庐 - After learning that her father Zheng Shen Zuo had died in battle, she divorced Li Yuanqing, shaved her head, went to Huai'an in the north of Qingzhou to retrieve her father's remains, and buried them together with her mother in the Jinxiang area of Xuxiu County, Yan state, then built a hut in the cemetery.
  • 识者曰女子适邉取父遗骸合烈而且孝诚可嘉也 - Those who understand said that the woman's act of going to the border to retrieve her father's remains and burying them together with her mother is both brave and filial, truly commendable.
  • 庐墓习于近俗国不能禁非也 - Building a hut over a grave is a recent custom, but it cannot be prohibited by the state, as it is not wrong.
  • 广引礼经而证之 - Extensively citing the rites to prove it.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 祖之道 - Refers to the ancestral way or tradition.
  • 素王 - A term for Confucius, often referred to as the 'Simple King' or 'King without a crown.'
  • 天竺 - The ancient Chinese name for India, where Buddhism originated.
  • 割爱中之至难 - Refers to the difficulty of renouncing worldly attachments, a key concept in Buddhist practice.

  • 不跻大位 - Means 'did not attain a high official position,' referring to Bai Juyi's lack of significant political advancement.

  • 既启之而复卷之 - Means 'opened it and then rolled it up again,' indicating a brief examination followed by immediate closure.
  • 不足久矣 - Means 'is not worth keeping for long,' expressing a dismissive attitude towards Bai Juyi's work.
  • 其文章精绝何必览焉 - Means 'his writing is so exquisite, why bother to read it?' indicating a dismissive attitude towards the value of reading Bai Juyi's work.
  • 其见抑也如此 - Means 'he is suppressed in this way,' indicating the extent of the suppression.
  • 咸曰有学士才非宰臣器识者 - Means 'all said that he has the talent of a scholar but lacks the vision of a prime minister,' indicating a mixed assessment of Bai Juyi's abilities.
  • 于其答制中见经纶之用 - Means 'in his response to imperial edicts, one can see his ability to manage state affairs,' highlighting Bai Juyi's administrative skills.
  • 为时所排 - Means 'was suppressed by the times,' indicating the political climate's impact on Bai Juyi.
  • 贾谊在汉文之朝不为卿相 - Refers to the historical figure Jia Yi (贾谊) who served under Emperor Wen of Han but did not become a high-ranking official, providing a historical parallel.
  • 葆光子曰 - Means 'Bao Guangzi said,' introducing a commentator or scholar's perspective.
  • 其不引翼义在于斯 - Means 'his reluctance to support them is due to this,' indicating a reason for Li Deyu's actions.
  • 非抑文章也 - Means 'it is not suppressing his writing,' clarifying the nature of the suppression.
  • 虑其朋比而掣肘也 - Means 'fearing that they would form alliances and hinder his actions,' indicating a strategic concern.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 益怒之 - 益 means 'more' or 'increasingly,' and 怒之 means 'to be angry with him.' Together, it conveys a growing or intensified anger.
  • 奏岐有才无行不宜与第 - 奏 means 'to report' or 'to accuse,' 有才无行 means 'talented but lacking in conduct,' and 不宜与第 means 'not suitable for promotion.' This phrase indicates a formal accusation against someone.
  • 防宣宗私行为温岐所忤 - 防 means 'to prevent' or 'to guard against,' 宣宗私行 refers to Emperor Xuānzōng's private actions, and 所忤 means 'offended' or 'displeased.' This suggests that Emperor Xuānzōng's private actions were offensive to someone.
  • 授方城尉 - 授 means 'to grant' or 'to appoint,' and 方城尉 refers to a military officer in Fāngchéng. This phrase indicates an official appointment.
  • 因知此恨人多积悔读南华第二篇 - 因知 means 'therefore know,' 此恨 means 'this resentment,' 人多积悔 means 'people accumulate regret,' and 读南华第二篇 refers to reading the second chapter of the Nánhuá (a text often associated with the Zhuangzi). This suggests that people's accumulated regret is related to reading a specific text.
  • 殊不展分 - 殊 means 'extremely' or 'very,' and 不展分 means 'not showing favor' or 'not being friendly.' This phrase suggests a lack of favor or friendliness.
  • 商隐憾之 - 商隐 refers to Li Shangyin, and 憾之 means 'to resent him.' This indicates that Li Shangyin resented someone.
  • 题防阁落句云 - 题 means 'to inscribe' or 'to write,' 防阁 refers to a guardhouse or a place of defense, and 落句云 means 'the last line says.' This suggests that someone wrote a poem in a guardhouse, and the last line is mentioned.
  • 郎君官重施行马东阁无因许再窥 - 郎君 refers to a young man or a noble, 官重施行马 means 'officials ride horses,' and 东阁无因许再窥 means 'the eastern pavilion does not allow another look.' This suggests that the young man, despite his high status, cannot enter the eastern pavilion again.
  • 官只止使下贠外也 - 官只止 means 'the official position only stops,' and 使下贠外 refers to a low-ranking official. This suggests that the official position only allows for a low rank.
  • 江东罗隐亦受知于绹 - 江东 refers to the region of Jiangdong, 罗隐 is the name of the person, and 受知于绹 means 'was known to Táo.' This indicates that Luò Yǐn was known to Táo.
  • 毕竟无成 - 毕竟 means 'after all' or 'in the end,' and 无成 means 'no success.' This suggests that despite being known, there was no success.
  • 有诗哭相国 - 有诗 means 'there is a poem,' and 哭相国 means 'mourning the chancellor.' This suggests that there is a poem mourning a chancellor.
  • 深恩无以报 - 深恩 means 'deep favor,' and 无以报 means 'cannot repay.' This suggests that deep favor cannot be repaid.
  • 底事是柴荆 - 底事 means 'what matter' or 'what affair,' and 柴荆 refers to a humble dwelling. This suggests that the matter is related to a humble dwelling.
  • 以三才子怨望 - 三才子 refers to three talented scholars, and 怨望 means 'resentment' or 'discontent.' This suggests that the resentment of the three talented scholars.
  • 即知绹之遗贤也 - 即知 means 'therefore know,' 绌 refers to Táo, 遗贤 means 'to neglect talented people.' This suggests that it is known that Táo neglected talented people.
  • 骆山人吿王庭凑 - 骆山人 refers to Lù Shānrén, and 告王庭凑 means 'to inform Wang Tíngcuō.' This suggests that Lù Shānrén informed Wang Tíngcuō.
  • 唐田宏正之领镇州三军杀之而立王庭凑 - 唐田宏正 refers to Táng Tián Hóngzhèng, 领镇州三军 means 'led the three armies of Zhènzhōu,' 杀之 means 'killed him,' and 立王庭凑 means 'established Wang Tíngcuō.' This suggests that Táng Tián Hóngzhèng led the three armies of Zhènzhōu, killed someone, and established Wang Tíngcuō.
  • 即王武俊支属也 - 即 means 'is,' 王武俊 refers to Wáng Wǔjùn, 支属 means 'branch family' or 'relative.' This suggests that Wang Tíngcuō is a relative of Wáng Wǔjùn.
  • 庭凑生于别墅 - 庭凑 refers to Wang Tíngcuō, 生于 means 'born in,' 别墅 means 'a villa.' This suggests that Wang Tíngcuō was born in a villa.
  • 尝有鸠数十只朝集庭树暮集檐下 - 尝 means 'once,' 鸠 refers to a kind of bird, 数十只 means 'several dozen,' 朝集庭树 means 'gathered in the courtyard trees in the morning,' 暮集檐下 means 'gathered under the eaves in the evening.' This suggests that several dozen birds gathered in the courtyard trees in the morning and under the eaves in the evening.
  • 有里人骆徳播异之 - 有里人 means 'there was a villager,' 骆徳播 refers to Lù Débō, 异之 means 'found it strange.' This suggests that a villager named Lù Débō found it strange.
  • 及长骈胁善隂符谷之书 - 及长 means 'when he grew up,' 骈胁 means 'broad shoulders,' 善隂符谷之书 means 'skilled in the books of Yin符 and Gu鬼.' This suggests that when he grew up, he had broad shoulders and was skilled in certain books.
  • 歴军职得士心 - 歴军职 means 'served in military positions,' 得士心 means 'gained the trust of the soldiers.' This suggests that he served in military positions and gained the trust of the soldiers.
  • 曾使河阳囬在中路以酒困寝于路隅 - 曾使 means 'once sent,' 河阳 refers to Héyáng, 囬 means 'return,' 在中路 means 'on the way back,' 以酒困 means 'drunk from alcohol,' 寝于路隅 means 'slept in a corner of the road.' This suggests that he was once sent to Héyáng, returned on the way, got drunk, and slept in a corner of the road.
  • 忽有一人荷防而过熟视之 - 忽有 means 'suddenly there was,' 一人 means 'a person,' 荷防 means 'carrying a basket,' 而过 means 'passed by,' 熟视之 means 'looked at him carefully.' This suggests that suddenly a person carrying a basket passed by and looked at him carefully.
  • 贵当列土非常人也 - 贵当 means 'should be,' 列土 means 'a fiefdom,' 非常人也 means 'not an ordinary person.' This suggests that he should be a fiefdom holder, not an ordinary person.
  • 仆者寤以告庭凑 - 仆者 means 'the servant,' 寤 means 'awoke,' 以告 means 'to tell,' 庭凑 refers to Wang Tíngcuō. This suggests that the servant woke up and told Wang Tíngcuō.
  • 庭凑驰数里及之致敬而问 - 庭凑 refers to Wang Tíngcuō, 驰数里 means 'rode several miles,' 及之 means 'caught up with,' 致敬而问 means 'paid respects and asked.' This suggests that Wang Tíngcuō rode several miles, caught up with someone, paid respects, and asked.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 龍而右如虎龍虎氣交王在今秋 - A compound phrase combining imagery and historical prediction, meaning 'the dragon on the right is like a tiger, the dragon and tiger qi (vital energy) intersect, and the king will reign in this autumn.'
  • 家之庭合有大树树及于堂是其兆也 - Literally means 'the family courtyard should have a big tree, the tree reaching the hall is a sign of this,' symbolizing a good omen for the family.
  • 果为三军扶立为留后 - Means 'indeed, the three armies supported him as the acting governor,' indicating a significant political event.
  • 庭树婆娑暗北舎矣 - Means 'the courtyard trees sway and darken the northern shed,' describing a physical scene with symbolic meaning.
  • 墅西飞龙山神庭凑往祭之 - Means 'the god of the Flying Dragon Mountain in the west of the villa, Court Gather, went to worship it,' indicating a religious ritual.
  • 将及祠百歩有人具冠冕折腰于庭凑及入庙神乃侧坐至今面东起宇尚存焉 - A complex sentence that describes a scene at the temple, meaning 'as he was about to reach the temple, a hundred steps away, someone wearing a hat and robe bowed to him; when he entered the temple, the god was sitting sideways, facing east, and the structure still stands to this day.'
  • 庭凑清俭公正忠于朝廷勤于军民 - Means 'Court Gather was clear, thrifty, fair, loyal to the court, and diligent in serving the military and civilians.'
  • 子孙世嗣为镇帅至朱梁时王镕封赵王为部将张文礼灭之 - Means 'his descendants succeeded him as the commander of the garrison, and during the time of Zhu Liang, Wang Rong was enfeoffed as the Prince of Zhao, but was killed by his subordinate Zhang Wenli.'
  • 授任致冦 - Means 'appointing officials leads to trouble,' a general statement about the consequences of poor appointments.
  • 唐马植相公曾镇安南安抚军民懐柔蛮獠废珠池尚俭素 - Means 'the Chancellor Ma Zhi of Tang once governed An Nan, pacified the military and civilians, treated the barbarians and savages kindly, abolished the pearl pool, and maintained frugality and simplicity.'
  • 李琢后镇是用法大酷军城逺出而属南蛮六七年间劳动兵役 - Means 'Li Zhuo later governed this region, using harsh laws, and the military city was far away and belonged to the southern barbarians, leading to labor and military service for six or seven years.'
  • 咸通七年髙骈复之先是荆徐间征役拒蛮人甚苦之 - Means 'in the seventh year of Xiantong, Gao Pian restored it; previously, between Jing and Xu, the conscription and resistance to the barbarians were very difficult.'
  • 有举子闻许卒二千没于蛮乡有诗刺曰 - Means 'a candidate heard that two thousand soldiers had died in the barbarian territory, and wrote a poem to criticize, saying...' followed by a long and complex poem that criticizes the appointment of officials, leading to suffering and death in the southern regions.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 放孤寒三人及第 - Promoted three isolated and poor scholars to pass the imperial examination; '孤寒' refers to scholars who are isolated and poor, and '及第' means to pass the examination.
  • 科松防花事附 - A phrase that seems to be a title or a note, possibly referring to a specific event or context related to the promotion of the scholars.
  • 咸通中礼部侍郎髙湜知举榜内孤贫 - During the Xiantong era, the Minister of Rites, Gao Shi, was in charge of the imperial examination and promoted some isolated and poor scholars.
  • 人多书于屋壁 - Many people wrote their poems on the walls of houses, indicating the popularity and visibility of the poems.
  • 许棠有洞庭诗尤工 - Xu Tang had a poem about Dongting Lake that was especially skillful; '尤工' means 'especially skillful.'
  • 聂夷中河南中都人少贫苦精于古体 - Nie Yizhong, from Zhongdu in Henan, was poor when young and excelled in ancient poetry; '少贫苦' means 'poor when young' and '精于古体' means 'excellent in ancient poetry.'
  • 公子家诗云种花于西园花发青楼道 - A poem about a nobleman's house says, 'Planting flowers in the west garden, the flowers bloom along the green pavilion road'; '公子家诗' means 'a poem about a nobleman's house' and '种花于西园花发青楼道' is a poetic description.
  • 去之为恶草 - Removing them to become weeds; '去之' means 'removing them' and '恶草' means 'weeds.'
  • 咏田家诗云父耕原上田子斸山下荒 - A poem about a farmer's family says, 'The father tills the fields on the plain, the son clears the mountains below'; '咏田家诗' means 'a poem about a farmer's family' and '父耕原上田子斸山下荒' is a poetic description.
  • 六月禾未秀官家已修仓 - In June, the grain has not yet ripened, but the government has already built granaries; '六月禾未秀' means 'in June, the grain has not yet ripened' and '官家已修仓' means 'the government has already built granaries.'
  • 谁念盘中飡粒粒皆辛苦 - Who thinks about the effort put into each grain on the plate; '谁念' means 'who thinks about' and '盘中飡粒粒皆辛苦' means 'each grain on the plate is the result of hard work.'
  • 二月卖新丝五月粜新谷 - Selling new silk in February and new grain in May; '二月卖新丝' means 'selling new silk in February' and '五月粜新谷' means 'selling new grain in May.'
  • 医得眼前疮剜却心头肉 - Healing a surface wound by cutting out the heart; '医得眼前疮' means 'healing a surface wound' and '剜却心头肉' means 'cutting out the heart.'
  • 我愿君王心化为光明烛 - I wish the king's heart could transform into a bright candle; '我愿君王心' means 'I wish the king's heart' and '化为光明烛' means 'could transform into a bright candle.'
  • 不照绮罗筵只照逃亡屋 - Not illuminating the luxurious banquets but only the houses of the fleeing; '不照绮罗筵' means 'not illuminating the luxurious banquets' and '只照逃亡屋' means 'only the houses of the fleeing.'
  • 言近意逺合三百篇之防也 - The words are close but the meaning is far, combining the essence of the Three Hundred Poems; '言近意逺' means 'the words are close but the meaning is far' and '合三百篇之防' means 'combining the essence of the Three Hundred Poems.'
  • 盛得三人见湜之公道也 - Promoting three people to show Gao Shi's fairness; '盛得三人' means 'promoting three people' and '见湜之公道' means 'showing Gao Shi's fairness.'
  • 葆光子尝有同寮示我调举时诗卷内一句云科松为防花 - Bao Guangzi once had a colleague show me a line from a poem collection during the examination period, saying 'pine trees are guarded by flowers'; '葆光子' is a name, '尝有同寮示我' means 'once had a colleague show me,' '调举时诗卷内一句云' means 'a line from a poem collection during the examination period,' and '科松为防花' means 'pine trees are guarded by flowers.'
  • 贾浪仙云空庭唯有竹闲地拟栽松 - Jia Langxian said, 'In an empty courtyard, there are only bamboo; in idle land, pine trees are planted'; '贾浪仙' is a name, '云' means 'said,' '空庭唯有竹' means 'in an empty courtyard, there are only bamboo,' and '闲地拟栽松' means 'in idle land, pine trees are planted.'

  • 知其连州【一作山】人朝无强援 - Knowing that the person from Lianzhou (or a mountain) had no powerful supporters in court.
  • 谓僧曰某虽闲废能为此人致宰相 - Told the monk that although he was idle, he could still make this person a chancellor.
  • 尔后授河中少尹幕寮有贵族浮薄者蔑视之 - Later, when appointed as the Deputy Governor of Hedong, some arrogant and superficial nobles in his entourage despised him.
  • 浮薄幕客呼相国为尹公曰归朝作何官职 - The superficial and arrogant entourage members called the chancellor 'Governor' and asked, 'What position will you hold upon returning to court?'
  • 得路即作宰相 - As soon as I get a chance, I will become chancellor.
  • 此郎大笑之 - This man laughed at him.
  • 在席亦有异其言者 - There were also some who found his words unusual.
  • 自是以水部贠外知制诰相次入翰林以至大拜也 - From then on, he successively rose from Water Department Censor to Chief of Drafting Imperial Edicts, then into the Hanlin Academy, and finally received a high-ranking appointment.
  • 唐相国李公福河中永乐有宅庭槐一本抽三枝直过当舎屋一枝不及 - Duke Li Fu of the Tang Dynasty, who lived in Yongle, Hedong, had a courtyard with a槐tree that grew three branches, two of which reached over the house, but one did not.
  • 相国同堂昆弟三人曰石曰程皆登宰执唯福一人歴镇使相而已 - The chancellor had three brothers named Shi and Cheng, who all reached high positions, while Fu only held the position of military governor.
  • 近者石晋朝赵令公莹家庭有檽树婆娑异常四逺俱见有望气者诣其邻里问人云此家合有登宰辅者里叟曰无之然赵令先徳小字相之儿得非此应乎术士曰王气方盛不在身当其子孙尔后中令由太原判官大拜出将入相则前言果效矣 - Recently, during the Shizhou era, Zhao Linggong Ying had a枣tree in his family courtyard that was unusually graceful and visible from afar. A fortune-teller visited the neighborhood and asked people if this family would produce a chancellor. The elderly man said there was no such thing, but the fortune-teller suggested that Zhao Linggong's son, who was nicknamed 'Chancellor,' might fulfill this prophecy. The fortune-teller said that the auspicious signs were strong and would manifest in his descendants. Later, Zhao Linggong rose from a judge in Taiyuan to become a high-ranking official and a chancellor, proving the fortune-teller's prediction correct.
  • 凡士之宦逹非止一途或以才升或由命遇则盛衰之气亦随人而效之向者槐枣异常岂非王气先集耶不然何荣茂挺特拔耸之如是也 - Scholars who achieve success do so through various paths, either through talent or fate. The rise and fall of fortunes also follow people, and the unusual growth of the槐and枣trees must be a sign of auspiciousness. Otherwise, why would they grow so luxuriantly and uniquely?

This describes a humorous encounter.

Key points include: '当垆' - Means 'selling wine,' referring to a common occupation in ancient times; '不埽街官吏殴之' - Means 'officials who did not sweep the street beat him,' indicating a conflict with local authorities; '其母甚贤勉以进脩' - Means 'his mother was very virtuous and encouraged him to study,' highlighting her role in his education; '归乡以成名为期' - Means 'returning home to achieve fame,' indicating his goal; '每嵗糇粮纸笔衣服仆马皆自成都赍致' - Means 'every year, food, paper, pens, clothes, horses, and servants were sent from Chengdu,' showing the support he received; '郎中业八韵唯螳蜋赋大行太和元年及第' - Means 'Chen Fang's eight-verse essay on the mantis became widely known, and he passed the imperial examination in the first year of Taihe,' indicating his success; '李相固言览报状处分厢界下酒斾阖其户家人犹拒之逡巡贺登第乃圣善奬训之力也' - Means 'Prime Minister Li, upon seeing the announcement, ordered the removal of the wine sign and closed the door, but his family still refused, and eventually congratulated him on passing the examination, attributing it to his mother's encouragement,' indicating the mixed reactions and ultimate success; '后为白中令子壻西川副使连典彭汉两郡而终' - Means 'Later, he became the son-in-law of Bai Zhongling, served as a deputy governor of Xishu, and successively governed Peng and Han prefectures until his death,' indicating his career progression.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening simile governs the whole sentence and frames writing through the logic of horsemanship.
  • The concessive pattern acknowledges natural brilliance but emphasizes the need for restraint.
  • The final prohibition can be read as two linked dangers: straying from the proper track and letting the writing run off into structural disorder.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '成令公为蛇绕身' is elliptical and can be interpreted as 'Cheng Linggong was suddenly surrounded by a snake,' but the exact nature of the encounter is not clear.
  • The phrase '防至于殒' can be interpreted as 'prevented from dying' or 'saved from death,' but in this context, it suggests that Cheng Linggong was ultimately unable to escape his fate.
  • The phrase '茍有所负，死生唯命' can be interpreted as 'if there is something owed, life and death are determined by fate,' suggesting a sense of inevitability.
  • The phrase '逡巡蛇亦亡去' can be interpreted as 'he hesitated, and the snake also disappeared,' indicating a moment of indecision or delay.
  • The phrase '招辑户口，训练士卒，防流而镇渚宫' is a list of administrative and military tasks, but the exact order and relationship between the tasks are not clear.
  • The phrase '授节旄，抚绥凋残，励精为理' is a list of actions taken by Cheng Linggong, but the exact order and relationship between the actions are not clear.
  • The phrase '勤王奉国，通商务农' is a list of duties and responsibilities, but the exact order and relationship between the duties are not clear.
  • The phrase '骋辨陵人，又多矜伐' can be interpreted as 'he engaged in vigorous debate and challenged others, and he also boasted,' indicating a confrontational and arrogant personality.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '李逺贠外每吟其诗而思其人' describes how Li Yuan often recited Cao Tang's poetry and thought of him, but it can be read as a single, continuous action.
  • The sentence '昔者未睹标仪将谓可乘鸾' can be read as a conditional statement, implying that if Li Yuan had not seen Cao Tang, he would have thought him worthy of riding a celestial bird.
  • The sentence '始蒋伸相登庸李景逊尚书西川览报状而叹曰不能伏事斯人也' is a complex one, describing how Jiang Shen, after seeing the appointment of Li Jingxun, expressed disappointment and chose to resign.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 谒中尉便施谢酒之敬 - Visit the Central Commander and show respect through the offering of wine.
  • 中尉讶之 - The Central Commander was surprised by it.
  • 俄而宾主即席坐定 - Soon after, the host and guests took their seats.
  • 中尉白诸相曰 - The Central Commander said to the other officials.
  • 某与起居清浊异流 - I and the others have different social standings.
  • 曾蒙中外既虑玷辱何惮改更 - I have already been honored by both the inner and outer courts; how could I be afraid of changing?
  • 今日猥地谢酒即又不可 - Today, in a humble position, offering wine is not appropriate.
  • 张公惭惧交集 - Zhang Gong was filled with shame and fear.
  • 自此甚为羣彦薄之 - From then on, he was greatly disrespected by the scholarly elite.
  • 乐公举进士初陈启事谒李昭侍郎自媒云 - Le Gong, when he first took the imperial examination, submitted a petition to Li Zhaoshi, a high-ranking official, and recommended himself.
  • 别于九经书及老庄洎八都赋外着八百卷书请垂比试诚有学问也 - Except for the Nine Classics, the Laozi and Zhuangzi, and the Eight Capital Assignments, he wrote eight hundred volumes of books. Please allow me to be tested, for I truly have knowledge.
  • 然于制诰不甚简当时人或未可之 - But he was not very simple in his official documents, and some people of the time did not approve of it.
  • 薛少师拒中外事 - The story of Xue Shao, the Junior Tutor, rejecting the inner and outer courts.
  • 唐薛廷珪少师右族名流仕于衰世梁太祖兵力日强朝廷倾动渐自尊大天下惧之 - During the late Tang Dynasty, Xue Tinggui, the Junior Tutor, was a prominent figure from a noble family who served during a declining era. The First Emperor of Later Liang, Li Keyong, grew stronger in military power, causing the court to be shaken and gradually become more arrogant, which made the whole world fear him.
  • 孤卿为四镇官告使夷门客将刘翰先来防恐申中外孤卿佯言不防谓谒者曰某无徳安敢輙受令公拜竟不为屈 - Xue Tinggui, as the envoy for the four commanderies, was approached by Liu Han, a guest officer from Yimen, who came to prevent him from submitting to the inner and outer courts. Xue Tinggui pretended not to know and told the attendant, 'I am unworthy; how dare I accept the Grand Marshal's obeisance?' He refused to bow.
  • 洎受禅之后勉事于梁而太祖优容之夀考而终也 - After accepting the throne, he served Later Liang with dedication, and the First Emperor of Later Liang treated him with leniency, allowing him to live a long life until his death.
  • 中间奉命册蜀先主为司徒馆中旧疾发动蜀人送当医人杨仆俾攻疗之孤卿致书感谢其书末请借肩舆归京寻医 - During this period, he was ordered to confer the title of Grand Tutor on the First Emperor of Shu. His old illness flared up in the palace, and the people of Shu sent a doctor named Yang Pu to treat him. Xue Tinggui wrote a letter of thanks, and at the end of the letter, he asked for a sedan chair to return to the capital to seek medical treatment.
  • 蜀主讶之乃曰幸有方药何 - The First Emperor of Shu was surprised and said, 'Fortunately, there is effective medicine.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 告誓天地 - A formal declaration or oath made to heaven and earth, often used in solemn or religious contexts.
  • 非礼偶之 - Refers to an improper or dishonorable match, often in the context of marriage or alliance.
  • 每旦未视事前必伸问安之礼 - A ritual of asking for well-being before starting daily duties, emphasizing propriety and respect.
  • 厄在重围 - Being in a dire situation or under siege, often used to describe a difficult or dangerous predicament.
  • 遽为王蜀先主攻围保守孤城 - A rapid or sudden attack by the ruler of Shu (Liu Bei) on a besieged city, emphasizing urgency and military action.
  • 救兵不至十日而为西川所破而害焉 - Help arriving too late, leading to destruction within ten days, highlighting the consequences of delayed assistance.
  • 城克执之 - The city being captured and the person being taken prisoner, indicating a loss of control.
  • 冀为其用欲寛之 - Hoping to use the person, so wishing to treat them leniently, showing a pragmatic approach.
  • 某受杨司徒提拔不敢惜死 - I have been promoted by Yang, the Grand Tutor, and dare not hesitate to die, expressing loyalty and readiness to sacrifice.
  • 先主叹【一作嗟】赏而行戮 - The ruler expressing sorrow or admiration before executing, showing a mix of emotions.
  • 为设祭而之 - Setting up a memorial service and burying the person, indicating respect and closure.
  • 新理之所兵力未完 - The newly established regime's military forces are not yet fully prepared, highlighting a state of weakness.
  • 遽为王蜀先主攻围保守孤城 - A rapid or sudden attack by the ruler of Shu (Liu Bei) on a besieged city, emphasizing urgency and military action.
  • 救兵不至十日而为西川所破而害焉 - Help arriving too late, leading to destruction within ten days, highlighting the consequences of delayed assistance.
  • 成令公和州载 - A title or name, likely referring to a historical figure or event.
  • 唐天祐中淮师围武昌不解 - During the Tang Dynasty, in the Tianyou period, the Huai army besieged Wuchang without relief, indicating a prolonged military conflict.
  • 杜洪令公乞师于梁王 - Du Hong, the official, requested assistance from the King of Liang, showing a plea for help.
  • 梁王与荆方睦乃讽成令帅兵救之 - The King of Liang, being friendly with Jingzhou, advised Cheng to lead troops to rescue, indicating diplomatic relations.
  • 禀奉霸主欲亲征乃以巡属五州事力造巨舰一艘 - Following the hegemon's desire to personally lead the campaign, he ordered the construction of a large ship using resources from five prefectures, emphasizing grandeur and effort.
  • 三年而成号曰和州载 - Completed in three years, named 'Hezhou Zai,' indicating the ship's significance and duration of construction.
  • 舰上列防事洎司局有衙府之制 - The ship was equipped with defensive measures and had a structure resembling a government office, emphasizing its function and design.
  • 又有齐山截海之名 - It was also named 'Qishan Jiehai,' meaning 'Cutting through the Sea,' indicating its purpose and grandeur.
  • 其于华壮即可知也 - Its splendor and grandeur can be imagined, showing admiration for its appearance.
  • 饰非拒谏断自其意 - Concealing faults and rejecting advice, making decisions based on his own will, indicating authoritarianism.
  • 幕寮俛仰不措一词 - The officials in the government were unable to speak up, showing their helplessness or fear.
  • 唯孔目官杨厚赞成之 - Only the clerk Yang Hou supported him, indicating loyalty and support.
  • 舟次破军山下为呉师纵燎而焚之中令溺死兵士溃散 - The ship was attacked and burned by Wu forces at Pujun Mountain, leading to the drowning of the commander and the scattering of the soldiers, highlighting the disaster.
  • 先是改名曰汭汭字即水内也水内之死岂非前兆乎 - Previously, the name was changed to 'Rui,' which means 'inside the water,' and his death in the water was seen as a premonition, indicating superstition or foreboding.
  • 湖南及朗州军入江陵俘载军人百姓职掌伎巧僧道伶官并归长沙 - The armies of Hunan and Langzhou entered Jingzhou, capturing soldiers, civilians, and various officials, leading them to Changsha, indicating a military conquest and relocation.
  • 改汭之名和州之说盖前定也 - Changing the name 'Rui' and the story of 'Hezhou Zai' were all predestined, indicating a belief in fate or destiny.

Idiomatic Expressions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 屯难之世 - Refers to a time of great difficulty or turmoil, often used to describe a period of political or social instability.
  • 君子遭遇不幸 - Literally 'gentlemen encounter misfortune,' referring to the misfortunes faced by virtuous or noble individuals.
  • 钱尚父 - A title given to Qian Chu (钱镠), a powerful warlord who founded the Wu Kingdom. '尚父' means 'elder father' or 'paternal ancestor.'
  • 钱塘八都 - Refers to the eight districts of Qiantang (钱塘), which were under Qian Chu's control.
  • 董昌 - A historical figure, a rebel leader who was defeated by Qian Chu.
  • 奄有杭越 - A phrase meaning 'to completely occupy or control Hangzhou and Yue (浙江地区),' indicating Qian Chu's dominance over these regions.
  • 罹其笼罩 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'to be caught in his net' or 'to suffer under his control.'
  • 出于草莱 - A phrase meaning 'coming from the wilderness or common people,' indicating someone of humble origins.
  • 未谙事体 - A phrase meaning 'not familiar with the affairs or customs of the world,' suggesting a lack of experience in officialdom.
  • 重县宰而轻郎官 - A phrase meaning 'valuing county magistrates more than imperial court officials,' indicating a preference for local officials over those in the central government.
  • 某人非才只可作郎官不堪作县令 - A phrase meaning 'such a person is not capable of being a county magistrate, only fit to be an imperial court official,' suggesting a low opinion of the person's abilities.
  • 表奏孔目官 - A title for a low-ranking official responsible for drafting and managing official documents.
  • 拒而见笞 - A phrase meaning 'refused and was beaten,' indicating resistance followed by punishment.
  • 畏死禀命 - A phrase meaning 'fearing death and obeying orders,' indicating a submissive attitude.
  • 玉石俱焚 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'both jade and stone are burned together,' indicating that both good and bad are destroyed together.
  • 吁可惜也 - An exclamation meaning 'alas, how pitiful,' expressing regret or sorrow.
  • 归登尚书 - A proper name, referring to a historical figure.
  • 温饱而卒 - A phrase meaning 'died in comfort and with food and warmth,' indicating a peaceful and prosperous death.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生故吏而告之因 - Ambiguous phrase; could mean 'a former official who informed him' or 'a former official who told him.'
  • 别适人乱伦再醮自河东始也 - Complex phrase: '别适人' (bié shì rén) means 'to marry someone else,' '乱伦' (luàn lún) means 'incestuous relations,' and '再醮' (zài jiào) means 'remarriage.' Suggests a remarriage involving incestuous relations began in Hedong (河东).
  • 性甚鄙啬尝烂一羊脾旋割旋噉封其残者一旦内子于封处割食八座不见元封大怒其内由是没身不食肉斯亦愈于和峤之流也 - Describes a person's miserly behavior. '性甚鄙啬' (xìng shèn bǐ sè) means 'very stingy in nature,' '尝烂一羊脾' (cháng làn yī yáng pí) means 'once he spoiled a lamb's liver,' '旋割旋噉' (xuán gē xuán dàn) means 'cut and ate it immediately,' '封其残者' (fēng qí cán zhě) means 'sealed the leftovers,' and '一旦内子于封处割食八座不见元封大怒其内由是没身不食肉斯亦愈于和峤之流也' (yī dàn nèi zǐ yú fēng chù gē shí bā zuò bù jiàn yuán fēng dà nù qí nèi yóu shì méi shēn bù shí ròu sī yì yù yú hé qiáo zhī liú yě) describes a situation where his wife cut and ate the sealed leftovers, and he became very angry, leading him to never eat meat again. '斯亦愈于和峤之流也' (sī yì yù yú hé qiáo zhī liú yě) means 'this is better than the likes of He Qiao.'
  • 西川自唐刘辟搆逆后久无干戈人不习战每嵗诸道差兵屯戍大渡河蛮旗才举望风而溃咸通中长驱直抵府城居人有扃户而拒之蛮亦不敢扣门也 - Describes a historical event in Western Sichuan (西川) after the rebellion of Liu Pi (刘辟). '久无干戈' (jiǔ wú gān gē) means 'long without war,' '人不习战' (rén bù xí zhàn) means 'people are not accustomed to fighting,' '每嵗诸道差兵屯戍大渡河' (měi suì zhū dào chā bīng tún shù dà dù hé) means 'each year, troops are sent to garrison at the Dadu River,' '蛮旗才举望风而溃' (mán qí cái jǔ wàng fēng ér kuì) means 'as soon as the barbarian flag is raised, they flee at the sight of it,' and '咸通中长驱直抵府城居人有扃户而拒之蛮亦不敢扣门也' (xián tōng zhōng zhǎng qū zhí dǐ fǔ chéng jū rén yǒu jiōng hù ér jù zhī mán yì bù gǎn kòu mén yě) describes a situation during the Xiantong period where the barbarians advanced directly to the city, and the residents closed their doors to resist, and the barbarians did not dare to knock on the doors.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 性甚鄙啬尝烂一羊脾旋割旋噉封其残者一旦内子于封处割食八座不见元封大怒其内由是没身不食肉斯亦愈于和峤之流也 - Describes a person's miserly behavior and a specific incident that led to a change in their eating habits. '和峤之流' (hé qiáo zhī liú) refers to a historical figure known for his frugality.
  • 西川自唐刘辟搆逆后久无干戈人不习战每嵗诸道差兵屯戍大渡河蛮旗才举望风而溃咸通中长驱直抵府城居人有扃户而拒之蛮亦不敢扣门也 - Describes a historical event in Western Sichuan (西川) after the rebellion of Liu Pi (刘辟). The Xiantong period (咸通) is a specific era in Chinese history.

  • 某常负此妪八十万钱 - Means 'I often owe this old woman eighty thousand coins,' where '某' is a humble self-reference, '负' means to owe, and '妪' refers to an old woman.
  • 今方忍耻而偿之 - Means 'now I am enduring shame to repay her,' where '忍耻' means enduring shame, and '偿之' means to repay.

  • 自言事不验 - Means 'he claimed that the matter was not verified,' where '自言' means he claimed, and '不验' means not verified.
  • 神乃辞去 - Means 'the spirit then departed,' where '辞去' means to depart.
  • 梁相国李公琪传其事 - Means 'the chancellor Li Gong Qi of the Liang dynasty recorded this matter,' where '传其事' means to record the matter.
  • 嗟乎英特之士 - Means 'alas, a remarkable man,' where '英特' refers to remarkable or extraordinary.
  • 负一女子之债死且如是 - Means 'dying for the debt owed to a woman is already like this,' where '负一女子之债' means owing a debt to a woman, and '死且如是' means dying in such a manner.
  • 而况于负国之大债乎 - Means 'how much more so for a great national debt?' where '负国之大债' means owing a great national debt.
  • 窃君之禄而不报 - Means 'stealing the ruler's salary without reporting,' where '窃' means to steal, '君之禄' means the ruler's salary, and '不报' means without reporting.
  • 盗君之柄而不忠 - Means 'stealing the ruler's authority and being disloyal,' where '盗君之柄' means stealing the ruler's authority, and '不忠' means being disloyal.
  • 而崔相国出入将相殆三十年也 - Means 'and Chancellor Cui served as a general and prime minister for nearly thirty years,' where '出入将相' means serving as both general and prime minister, and '殆三十年' means nearly thirty years.

  • 位非忝窃 - The position is not undeserved; the position is not gained through improper means.
  • 潜结中禁 - Secretly form connections within the inner palace.
  • 大拜 - Promoted to a high position.

  • 希割 - Desire to divide or split.
  • 建定边军节度 - Establish the Governor of the Frontier Army.
  • 擢领此任 - Promoted to take this post.
  • 隂通 - Secretly communicate with.
  • 侵轶 - Intrude or invade.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 昭宗遂有此拜对敭之日面陈时事数条 - On the day of his appointment, Emperor Zhaozong personally presented several matters of state.
  • 每言臣为陛下致之 - Each time he said that he would bring it to the Emperor.
  • 洎操大柄无以施展 - When he took on great power, he could not implement his plans.
  • 自是恩泽日衰 - From then on, the emperor's favor gradually diminished.
  • 中外腾沸 - There was turmoil both inside and outside the court.
  • 内优曰俳优穆刀陵作念经行者 - The eunuchs said that the performer Mu Dao Ling was reciting scriptures.
  • 至御【一作狮】前曰若是朱相即是非相 - When he reached the emperor's presence, he said, 'If Zhu is a prime minister, then he is not a prime minister.'
  • 翌日出官时人曰拔士为相自古有也 - The next day, when he was dismissed from office, people said, 'It has always been possible for a scholar to be made prime minister.'
  • 君子【一作此】不耻其言之不出耻躬之不逮 - A gentleman does not despise words that are not spoken, but despises actions that fall short.
  • 况唐末防乱天下阻兵虽负奇才不能谋画而朱公一儒生以区区辩给欲整其乱祗自取辱焉 - Especially in the late Tang, when the country was in turmoil and everyone was armed, even with extraordinary talent, one could not plan effectively. Zhu Gong, a mere scholar, tried to restore order with his limited eloquence and only brought himself disgrace.
  • 涓缕未申而教乐僮吹筚篥甚为识者所责也 - Before he could fully express his ideas, he was criticized by knowledgeable people for having his young attendants play the hululu.
  • 李羣玉轻薄事【韦沆李璩附】 - The frivolous affairs of Li Qunyu [with references to Wei Hang and Li Qu].
  • 唐李羣玉校书字文山澧州人有诗名散逸不乐应举亲友强之一上而已 - Li Qunyu, a scholar from Lizhou, had a reputation for poetry and was not fond of taking the imperial examinations. His friends and relatives forced him to take it once.
  • 然多狎酒徒疑其为张祜之流 - However, he often associated with drunkards, and it was suspected that he was like Zhang Hu.
  • 李少逢善夷谪官澧阳备知其行止 - Li Shao, who was exiled to Lizyang, knew all about his behavior.
  • 李谓艾侯曰小子困甚 - Li said to Ai, 'I am in dire straits.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 幸使君痛救之 - A plea for urgent assistance, literally 'may the governor kindly rescue it.'
  • 州將以【一作戒】其輕脫 - The phrase '一作戒' indicates that '戒' (admonish) is an alternative reading. The phrase can be interpreted as 'the governor should admonish for its recklessness' or 'the governor should warn against its recklessness.'
  • 所濟不厚也 - Means 'the benefit is not great,' suggesting that the action taken was insufficient.
  • 流落不偶 - Means 'wandering and not meeting with good fortune,' describing someone who is unsuccessful.
  • 福相之曽孫也 - Refers to a person as the great-grandson of someone with a good fortune or reputation.
  • 衣缨旧族 - Refers to an old, prestigious family, often associated with high social status.
  • 攻古文 - Means 'to study or write in the classical style,' often referring to a scholarly pursuit.
  • 垂秦妇吟障子 - Refers to hanging a screen with the poem 'Qin Woman's Lament' on it, a practice that could be seen as a form of self-promotion.
  • 好事不出门恶事行千里 - A proverb meaning 'good deeds don't spread far, but bad deeds travel a thousand miles,' emphasizing the spread of negative information.
  • 士君子得不戒之乎 - A rhetorical question meaning 'how can scholars and gentlemen not be cautious about it?'

  • 晋相和凝 - Refers to He Ning, a chancellor from Jin (a historical dynasty).

  • 内库烧为锦绣灰天街踏尽公卿骨 - A line from 'Qin Woman's Lament,' describing the destruction and chaos of the rebellion.
  • 曲子词 - Refers to lyrics or songs, often associated with entertainment and popular culture.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 出伦之才 - Refers to exceptional talent that stands out from the crowd.
  • 时辈妬忌 - Means 'envy and jealousy from contemporaries,' indicating that the individual's talent was resented by peers.
  • 罹于非横 - Means 'suffered from unjust misfortune,' referring to the unfair or violent events that befell the person.
  • 平生着文 - Means 'wrote throughout his life,' indicating a consistent literary output.
  • 百家着诸心要 - Refers to a collection of writings that distill the essence of various schools of thought.
  • 品流志五卷 - Means 'a five-volume work on social ranks and classifications.'
  • 易之心要三卷 - Refers to a three-volume work on the essence of the *I Ching* (Book of Changes).
  • 注论语一部 - Means 'a commentary on the *Analects*.'
  • 义说一篇 - Means 'a single work on the interpretation of principles.'
  • 阳春白雪 - A metaphor for highbrow or refined art that is appreciated only by a few.
  • 葆光子曰 - A title or attribution, possibly indicating a commentary or statement by a figure named 'Bao Guangzi.'
  • 陵轹荀孟 - Means 'overwhelm or surpass Xunzi and Mencius,' indicating that the writer's style was superior to these classical philosophers.
  • 穅秕顔谢 - Refers to 'chaff and husks of Yan and Xie,' metaphorically indicating that the writer's work was superior to the works of Yan and Xie (likely referring to Yan Zhitui and Xie Lingyun).
  • 寂寂 - Means 'silent' or 'unheard of,' indicating that the writer's work was not widely recognized.
  • 俗誉喧喧者 - Means 'those who are loudly praised by the masses,' indicating that the passage is criticizing popular acclaim.
  • 滥吹 - Means 'false praise' or 'empty flattery,' indicating that the passage is criticizing superficial or unwarranted praise.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 内官 - Refers to eunuchs who served in the imperial court, often in positions of power.
  • 改创职事 - Means 'to reform or create new duties,' indicating changes in the roles and responsibilities of eunuchs.
  • 窦给事附 - '窦给事' likely refers to a specific eunuch named Dou, and '附' means 'to append' or 'to add,' suggesting additional information about him.
  • 擅权专制 - Means 'to usurp power and rule autocratically,' describing the actions of powerful eunuchs.
  • 忠孝 - Refers to loyalty and filial piety, traditional Confucian virtues.
  • 简编 - Means 'to be recorded in history books,' indicating that some eunuchs were noted for their loyalty and filial piety.
  • 兵难荐臻 - Means 'military disasters repeatedly occurred,' describing the frequent military crises during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 天子播越 - Means 'the emperor was forced to flee,' indicating the instability of the imperial court.
  • 亲卫戎柄 - Refers to the military command of the emperor's personal guard.
  • 鱼朝恩 - A prominent eunuch during the Tang Dynasty who held significant power.
  • 窦文塲 - Another powerful eunuch during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 拟于四相 - Means 'comparable to the four chancellors,' indicating the high status of these eunuch positions.
  • 十六宫使 - Refers to sixteen palace officials, often eunuchs.
  • 卿寺之职 - Means 'official duties of the ministries,' indicating the roles of eunuchs in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 权为班行备员 - Means 'acting as a formal member of the bureaucracy,' suggesting that eunuchs held ceremonial or nominal positions.
  • 供奉官紫绶 - Refers to officials with purple belts, a sign of high rank.
  • 入侍后军容使 - Means 'to serve in the imperial court as a military commander.'
  • 杨复恭 - A powerful eunuch during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 襴笏宣导 - Refers to the use of ceremonial robes and tablets to announce or guide, indicating a formal role in the court.
  • 改作 - Means 'to change or reform,' suggesting that Elong's role was altered.
  • 严遵美 - A eunuch known for his excellent service.
  • 内谒之最良也 - Means 'the best eunuch for internal court affairs,' indicating his high status.
  • 典戎 - Means 'to command the military,' suggesting that he held a military position.
  • 唐末致仕 - Means 'retired at the end of the Tang Dynasty,' indicating his retirement.
  • 居蜀郡 - Means 'lived in Shu Prefecture,' indicating his place of residence after retirement.
  • 鄙叟庸夫 - Means 'a common old man,' indicating his humble status after retirement.
  • 时得亲狎 - Means 'occasionally close to him,' suggesting that he was occasionally approached or befriended.
  • 书记所谢之语 - Refers to the words of thanks recorded in the official documents.
  • 折腰 - Means 'to bend the waist,' a gesture of respect or submission.
  • 行膝 - Means 'to kneel,' another gesture of respect or submission.
  • 汗流喘乏 - Means 'sweating profusely and panting,' indicating his physical state.
  • 伏以军容寂无所道 - Means 'to bow and say nothing about the military commander,' indicating his response to the eunuch's request.
  • 抵掌视之 - Means 'to slap the palm and look at it,' a gesture of impatience or dismissal.
  • 貌寝人微 - Means 'ugly and of low status,' indicating his appearance and social standing.
  • 凡事无能 - Means 'unable to do anything,' suggesting that he was ineffective.
  • 不敢 - Means 'not dare,' indicating his response to the eunuch's request.
  • 退而大咍 - Means 'laughed loudly after stepping back,' indicating his reaction to the eunuch's behavior.
  • 严公物故 - Means 'the death of the eunuch,' indicating his passing.
  • 蜀朝册赠 - Means 'bestowed by the Shu court,' indicating the posthumous honors given to him.
  • 命给事中 - Means 'appointed as a Censor,' indicating his official title.
  • 窦雍 - A eunuch who refused to accept the posthumous appointment.
  • 偏霸之世 - Means 'during the era of divided rule,' a period of political fragmentation.
  • 不茍且 - Means 'not to compromise,' indicating a principled stance.
  • 士人多之 - Means 'many scholars admired him,' indicating the respect he received from the scholarly community.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 内官 - Eunuchs were a significant political force in the Tang Dynasty, often holding powerful positions in the imperial court.
  • 鱼朝恩 - A powerful eunuch who played a significant role during the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 窦文塲 - Another influential eunuch who held high positions in the Tang court.
  • 十六宫使 - These were palace officials who often held important roles in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 卿寺之职 - Refers to the duties of the various ministries in the Tang court.
  • 供奉官紫绶 - High-ranking officials in the Tang court, often eunuchs.
  • 入侍后军容使 - A position in the imperial court responsible for military affairs.
  • 杨复恭 - A powerful eunuch who held significant influence in the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 襴笏宣导 - Formal court rituals involving the use of ceremonial robes and tablets.
  • 严遵美 - A eunuch known for his excellent service and high status.
  • 唐末致仕 - The end of the Tang Dynasty, a period of political turmoil and decline.
  • 居蜀郡 - Shu Prefecture, a region in southwestern China that became a center of power during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.
  • 鄙叟庸夫 - A common old man, reflecting the eunuch's humble status after retirement.
  • 时得亲狎 - Occasionally close to him, indicating that he was occasionally approached or befriended.
  • 书记所谢之语 - Official documents recording words of thanks.
  • 折腰 - A gesture of respect or submission.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 舍己俸三百千 - To set aside one's own salary, three hundred thousand (likely referring to coins or currency).
  • 剰三十千入已 - Remaining thirty thousand into one's own possession; describes the act of keeping a portion of the money for oneself.
  • 物故 - An archaic term meaning 'to pass away' or 'to die.'
  • 邻于腐坏 - '邻于' means 'close to' or 'on the verge of,' and '腐坏' means 'decay' or 'rot.' Describes the state of being close to decay.
  • 灌汤药以辅之 - '灌汤药' means 'to administer medicine,' and '以辅之' means 'to assist or support.' Describes the act of giving medicine to aid recovery.
  • 鬻衣鉢而偿之 - '鬻' means 'to sell,' '衣鉢' refers to the monk's robe and begging bowl (symbolic of monastic life), and '偿之' means 'to repay.' Describes the act of selling one's monastic possessions to repay a debt.
  • 栗爆烧氊破猫跳触鼎翻 - '栗爆' means 'chestnuts exploding,' '烧氊破' means 'burning felt and breaking,' '猫跳' means 'cat jumping,' '触' means 'touching,' and '鼎翻' means 'cauldron overturning.' Describes a chaotic scene involving chestnuts, felt, a cat, and a cauldron.
  • 投谒公卿 - '投谒' means 'to visit' or 'to pay respects,' and '公卿' refers to high-ranking officials. Describes the act of visiting high-ranking officials.
  • 猫儿狗子 - A colloquial expression meaning 'cats and dogs,' used here in a humorous or dismissive way.
  • 同列戏之 - '同列' means 'colleagues' or 'peers,' and '戏之' means 'to mock or tease.' Describes the act of colleagues teasing someone.
  • 不同文赋易为是者之乎 - '不同文赋' means 'not the same as literary compositions,' '易为' means 'easy to be,' '是者' means 'this one,' and '之乎' is a particle used to form a question or emphasis. Likely refers to the idea that something is not the same as literary compositions and is easy to be this one or that one.
  • 数日而罢 - '数日' means 'several days,' and '罢' means 'to dismiss' or 'to end.' Describes the act of being dismissed after several days.

  • 朝士览之不测涯涘 - Indicates that officials who read Li Shi's works could not fathom their depth.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 王超牋奏 - Refers to Wang Chao's memorial or official document; '牋奏' is a term for formal written submissions to the emperor.
  • 推奉李茂貞 - Means 'to support or follow Li Maozhen,' referring to Wang Chao's allegiance to the warlord Li Maozhen.
  • 恣意翺翔 - Means 'to freely soar or act with unrestrained freedom,' describing Wang Chao's bold and independent behavior.
  • 掌記 - Refers to the official responsible for drafting memorials and documents.
  • 掌記辟 - Means 'to appoint as the official in charge of memorials,' indicating Wang Chao's role in drafting official documents.
  • 權變之間 - Means 'in matters of strategy or flexibility,' referring to situations requiring adaptability.
  • 未甚惬防 - Means 'not entirely satisfactory or appropriate,' indicating that Wang Chao's performance was not fully satisfactory.
  • 切露直致 - Means 'frank and straightforward,' describing the style of Wang Baohui's writing.
  • 易為曉悟 - Means 'easy to understand,' indicating that Wang Baohui's writing was clear and accessible.
  • 鳯翔用王超牋奏 - Refers to the use of Wang Chao's memorials in the state of Fengxiang.
  • 思偶风云 - Means 'to seek to match the grandeur of the times,' indicating Wang Chao's ambition to align with the powerful.
  • 每遇飛章言伪而辩 - Means 'every time he submitted a memorial with false but eloquent arguments,' describing Wang Chao's deceptive yet persuasive writing style.
  • 蜀先主愛之 - Means 'the ruler of Shu loved him,' referring to Liu Bei's admiration for Wang Chao.
  • 以二王书题表藳示长乐公 - Means 'to show the drafts of the memorials written by the two Wangs to the Prince of Changle,' indicating the sharing of Wang Chao's work with the Prince of Changle.
  • 致书逊谢 - Means 'to write a letter of humility and apology,' describing the Prince of Changle's response.
  • 倍加赞赏 - Means 'to praise greatly,' indicating the Prince of Changle's high regard for Wang Chao's work.
  • 有眼未见有耳未闻 - Means 'to have eyes that have not seen and ears that have not heard,' a phrase used to criticize someone for being out of touch with reality.
  • 失事君去就 - Means 'to lose the ability to serve or leave a position appropriately,' indicating a failure in one's duty or role.
  • 興元留后 - Refers to the position of acting governor in Xingyuan.
  • 遇害 - Means 'to be killed,' indicating Wang Chao's tragic end.
  • 鳯鸣集三十卷 - Refers to Wang Chao's collection of thirty volumes titled 'Fengming Ji.'
  • 體效其筆 - Means 'to imitate his style,' referring to Shi Qinru's emulation of Wang Chao's writing.
  • 判官 - Refers to an official in charge of administrative matters.
  • 隨轩降蜀 - Means 'to follow and surrender to Shu,' indicating Shi Qinru's decision to join Shu.
  • 不能谦退逺害 - Means 'unable to remain humble and avoid harm,' describing Shi Qinru's failure to stay out of danger.
  • 宾主争露锋颖 - Means 'the host and guest both reveal their sharpness,' indicating a mutual display of skill or ambition.
  • 竟同诛之 - Means 'they were eventually executed together,' describing the tragic end of both Shi Qinru and his host.
  • 阅其缄题表章 - Means 'to read his sealed memorials and official documents,' indicating the examination of Shi Qinru's work.
  • 行行然 - Means 'step by step' or 'gradually,' describing a process or progression.
  • 宜其见忌而取祸也 - Means 'it is appropriate that he was feared and suffered misfortune,' indicating that Shi Qinru's fate was justified by his actions.
  • 初背荆州成中令降蜀 - Means 'initially defected from Jingzhou Governor Cheng Zhongling to Shu,' describing Xu Cun's initial defection.
  • 有意杀之 - Means 'had the intention to kill him,' indicating Liu Bei's plan to eliminate Xu Cun.
  • 亲吏栁脩业劝其谦静 - Means 'a close official named Liu Xiuye advised him to remain humble and quiet,' describing Liu Xiuye's advice to Xu Cun.
  • 每立大功而皆托疾 - Means 'every time he established a great achievement, he would feign illness,' indicating Xu Cun's strategy to avoid recognition.
  • 获免于先主之世 - Means 'escaped being killed during the reign of the ruler of Shu,' describing Xu Cun's survival.
  • 即彭城之旧寮 - Means 'like an old official from Pengcheng,' indicating a comparison to a former official from Pengcheng.
  • 不若髙阳之小吏矣 - Means 'not as good as a minor official from Gaoyang,' indicating a contrast to a minor official from Gaoyang.

  • 得虽然曽闻其草角觝牒词动乃数幅讥其无简当体要之用也 - Complex phrase: 'Although it is obtained, I have heard that the draft is extensive but lacks conciseness and relevance; criticizing it for lacking brevity, appropriateness, and essentiality.'
  • 天静则星辰可摘 - Metaphorical expression: 'If the sky is calm, the stars can be picked, implying a state of perfect harmony and clarity.'

  • 风言沸腾 - '风言' (fēng yán) is a set phrase for 'rumors' or 'gossip.' '沸腾' (fèi téng) is a metaphor for 'boiling' or 'spreading wildly.' Together, it suggests that rumors or slander are spreading rapidly.
  • 尺水丈波 - A classical metaphor for how small events can lead to large consequences. '尺' (chǐ) is an ancient unit of length (about 10 inches), '丈' (zhàng) is 10 '尺' (about 10 feet). '水' (shuǐ) is 'water,' '波' (bō) is 'wave.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 具酒馔素钱以祭之 - Ritual offering; '具' means to prepare, '酒馔' refers to food and drink, '素钱' means white money, '祭之' means to offer it as a sacrifice.

  • 我射着其脚必死也 - '我射着其脚' means I will shoot his foot, '必死也' means he will certainly die.
  • 俄而【抄本有一中字】 - '俄而' means soon after or shortly thereafter, note in brackets suggests a missing character in the manuscript.
  • 杨中尉暴染脚疾而殂 - '杨中尉' refers to a military officer named Yang, '暴染' means suddenly contracted, '脚疾' means foot disease, '殂' means to die.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 賔客有酒徒李坚白者麄有文笔 - This sentence introduces a guest who is a wine companion and has rough literary talent. The structure is straightforward but requires careful translation to maintain the flow.
  • 足下何以名为坚白 - A formal question asking why the person is named 'Jianbai.' The structure is clear but requires a respectful tone.
  • 莫要改为士元亮君雄是权耶 - A complex response to the question about the name. The structure is somewhat ambiguous but can be clarified by breaking it down.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 曰何不早见示风疾已深矣 - This sentence can be read as a question or a statement, depending on the context. It could mean 'Why wasn't this shown earlier? The wind disease has already become severe.'
  • 请速归处置家事委顺而已 - This sentence can be read as a single request or a sequence of actions. It could mean 'Please return quickly to handle family matters and submit to fate.'
  • 朝士闻而惶遽告退策马而归 - This sentence is a sequence of actions, where the officials heard, panicked, withdrew, and rode back home.
  • 只有一法请官人剰吃消梨 - This sentence can be read as a single statement or a sequence of instructions. It could mean 'There is only one method: please eat more pears.'
  • 或希万一 - This phrase can be read as a standalone statement or a part of a larger sentence. It could mean 'Perhaps there is a chance.'

  • 此僧惊惧因谬授其法并成药数两释缚而窜 - '惊惧' (fear and shock) and '谬授' (erroneously teach) describe the monk's reaction and subsequent actions.

  • 他日药尽遽属更变伶俜而已偶免谬妄之诛也 - '遽属更变' (suddenly change) and '伶俜' (lonely and destitute) describe the husband's subsequent state.
  • 彭韬光者与何生切邻兼得其事为余话之 - '切邻' (close neighbor) and '得其事' (learn the story) describe the relationship and knowledge of the narrator.

  • 许之术 - The art of physiognomy, where '许之术' is a specific term for fortune-telling.
  • 隆准龙顔 - High nose and dragon-like face, a traditional description of a noble or distinguished appearance.
  • 垂手过膝 - Hands hanging down past the knees, indicating a tall stature.
  • 必致非常 - Would lead to something extraordinary, where '必致' means 'would lead to' and '非常' means 'extraordinary.'
  • 黄寇犯阙 - Yellow bandits invaded the palace, where '黄寇' refers to rebels or bandits and '犯阙' means 'invaded the palace.'
  • 幸蜀 - Fled to Shu, where '幸' means 'fled' and '蜀' refers to the region of Shu (modern-day Sichuan).
  • 士民挟持入京 - Citizens forced him into the capital, where '挟持' means 'forced' and '入京' means 'into the capital.'
  • 升含元殿 - Ascended to the Hanyuan Hall, where '含元殿' is a specific hall in the imperial palace.
  • 寻亦遇害 - Soon after he was killed, where '寻' means 'soon after' and '遇害' means 'was killed.'
  • 岂大人之相只为一升殿乎 - Could the great man's appearance only be for ascending a single hall? where '岂' means 'could' and '大人之相' means 'the appearance of a great man.'
  • 莫可知之 - It is impossible to know, where '莫' means 'not' and '可知' means 'can be known.'
  • 叹嗟不复言知人之鍳也 - He sighed and no longer spoke about the skill of knowing people, where '叹嗟' means 'sighed,' '不复言' means 'no longer spoke,' and '知人之鍳' means 'the skill of knowing people.'

  • 倐忽变生何以遏也 - Means 'suddenly, a crisis arises, how can it be stopped?'
  • 遽命斩其伪使 - Means 'immediately ordered the execution of the rebel envoy.'

  • 重荣迎之 - Means 'Wang Chongrong welcomed him.'
  • 潏前为京兆尹有惨酷之名时谓之堕叠 - Means 'Dou Yu, before being Jingzhao Governor, had a reputation for being cruel and was called 'Duo Die.'
  • 翌日集军校于庭谓曰 - Means 'the next day, he gathered the military officers in the courtyard and said.'

  • 不敢仰视乃跃马复由北门而出 - Means 'Dou Yu dared not look up and jumped on his horse, leaving through the north gate.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The title '韩建卖李巨川' suggests a narrative about Han Jian selling or betraying Li Juchuan.

  • The phrase '山南失守随致仕' indicates that after the loss of Shannan, Li Juchuan was dismissed from office.
  • The sentence '杨军容复恭与守亮同奔北投太原' describes the actions of Yang Jūnróng Fùgōng and Yang Shǒuliàng, who fled north to Taiyuan.
  • The phrase '导行者引出华州' refers to guides leading them out of Huazhou.
  • The sentence '韩建挫辱杨骂为奴以短褐防之毙于枯木' describes Han Jian's treatment of Yang Jūnróng Fùgōng, including humiliation and death.
  • The phrase '朱令公军次于华用张濬计先取韩建' describes Zhu Lingong's military actions and the strategy of Zhang Jùn.
  • The sentence '巨川为韩建副使' indicates that Li Juchuan was a deputy to Han Jian.
  • The phrase '朱公谓曰车驾西幸皆公所教也' has Zhu Lingong accusing Han Jian of teaching the emperor to flee west.
  • The phrase '寻奏移许昌' indicates that Han Jian was later moved to Xuancheng.
